---
title: "Projects"
---

# Projects

Below are selected academic and professional projects demonstrating my experience in machine learning, statistical modeling, and data analytics.

## Machine Learning & NLP: Movie Review Sentiment Analysis 

Developed machine learning models to classify sentiment in movie reviews using the SST-5 dataset. Extracted text-based features and implemented Logistic Regression and KNN classifiers to predict sentiment categories. Model performance was evaluated using cross-validation and ROC-AUC metrics. PCA and K-Means clustering were also applied to sentence embeddings to explore patterns in the text data.

**Tools:** Python, Scikit-learn, NLP, Logistic Regression, KNN, PCA, K-Means, Sentence Transformers

## Predictive Modeling for Aluminum Durability 

Built a machine learning classification model to predict whether rolled aluminum components would pass an extreme durability test using manufacturing process variables and alloy composition features. Conducted extensive exploratory data analysis on a dataset containing over 120,000 observations. Compared multiple models including Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost, selecting CatBoost due to the lowest log-loss. PCA and K-Means clustering were used to represent alloy composition interactions, and Optuna was used for hyperparameter tuning. The final ensemble model achieved a 0.421 log-loss and ranked 4th place on the leaderboard.

**Tools:** Python, CatBoost, XGBoost, LightGBM, Random Forest, PCA, K-Means, Optuna, Scikit-learn

## Crime Pattern Analysis in Los Angeles 

Analyzed crime data from the LAPD to investigate how crime patterns changed before, during, and after COVID-19. Conducted exploratory data analysis to study trends in crime timing, geographic distribution, and shifts in crime categories. Applied statistical methods including multinomial logistic regression and visualizations to examine how crime types and locations evolved across time periods.

**Tools:** R, Data Visualization, Statistical Modeling, Multinomial Logistic Regression, Exploratory Data Analysis

## Student Awareness Analysis for Wescom Credit Union

Analyzed survey and operational data to understand awareness and perceptions of Wescom Credit Union among UCLA students. Used Snowflake to query and manage datasets and Power BI to build interactive dashboards visualizing student banking behavior, brand recognition, and engagement patterns. The results were used to propose data-driven strategies to improve Wescom’s visibility and engagement with UCLA students.

**Tools:** SQL, Snowflake, Power BI, Data Analysis, Business Analytics
